content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. He wants to sort it in non-decreasing order. Unfortunately, he can only modify it using specific operations. In each operation, Chef should do the following:


Choose an integer KKK such that 0<K<2300 \lt K \lt 2^{30}0<K<230.
Choose an integer PPP such that 1≤P≤N1 \leq P \leq N1≤P≤N.
Perform one of the following actions:

type t=1t = 1t=1: for each iii (1≤i≤P1 \le i \le P1≤i≤P), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai+KA_i+KAi​+K
type t=2t = 2t=2: for each iii (P≤i≤NP \le i \le NP≤i≤N), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai+KA_i+KAi​+K
type t=3t = 3t=3: for each iii (1≤i≤P1 \le i \le P1≤i≤P), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai⊕KA_i \oplus KAi​⊕K
type t=4t = 4t=4: for each iii (P≤i≤NP \le i \le NP≤i≤N), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai⊕KA_i \oplus KAi​⊕K


Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR. In different operations, Chef may choose different values of ttt, KKK and PPP.

Can you help Chef make the sequence non-decreasing using at most ⌊N/2⌋\lfloor N/2 \rfloor⌊N/2⌋ operations (possibly 000 times)? Find one such sequence of operations.

Note that you do not need to minimise the number of operations. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them. It is guaranteed that a solution using at most ⌊N/2⌋\lfloor N/2 \rfloor⌊N/2⌋ operations always exists.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then for each test case: the first line contains an integer N (3 ≤ N ≤ 1000). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN (0 ≤ Ai < 2^30).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output an integer X (0 ≤ X ≤ ⌊N/2⌋) – the number of operations you will perform. Then output X lines, each containing three integers t, P and K describing one operation (1 ≤ t ≤ 4, 1 ≤ P ≤ N, 0 < K < 2^30). After applying the operations in the given order the array must become non‑decreasing.

solution.approach:
We first sort a copy of the original array and obtain the target non‑decreasing sequence B. We then transform the original array into B using at most ⌊N/2⌋ operations. The key observation is that a suffix operation (type 2 or type 4) changes only elements with index ≥ P, therefore if we process the array from right to left we never disturb elements that are already fixed. For each position i from N down to N‑⌊N/2⌋+1 we compute the difference d = B[i] − A[i] (mod 2^30). If d ≠ 0 we perform a type 2 operation on the suffix starting at i with K = d, which adds d to every element A[i…N]. This makes A[i] equal to B[i] while only affecting positions that are still to be processed. After handling the last ⌊N/2⌋ positions the whole suffix A[N‑⌊N/2⌋+1 … N] already equals the target. The remaining prefix of length ≤ ⌊N/2⌋ is already non‑decreasing because it consists of the smallest ⌊N/2⌋ values of B, and any addition performed on the suffix never changes the relative order inside the prefix. Consequently the whole array becomes sorted using exactly the number of non‑zero suffix additions, which is never larger than ⌊N/2⌋. If the array is already sorted we output 0 operations.

The algorithm works in O(N log N) per test case because of the sorting step; all other work is linear. Memory usage is O(N).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case (dominated by sorting).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) – the original array and its sorted copy.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        A = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        B = sorted(A)
        limit = N // 2
        ops = []
        for i in range(N-1, N-limit-1, -1):
            cur = A[i]
            want = B[i]
            diff = (want - cur) & ((1 << 30) - 1)
            if diff:
                ops.append((2, i+1, diff))
                for j in range(i, N):
                    A[j] = (A[j] + diff) & ((1 << 30) - 1)
        out_lines.append(str(len(ops)))
        for t,p,k in ops:
            out_lines.append(f"{t} {p} {k}")
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
